records.ety: [L. completus, p. p. of complere to fill up; com- + plere to fill. See Full, a., and cf. Comply, Compline.]

defs: Filled up; with no part or element lacking; free from deficiency; entire; perfect; consummate.
quotes: That thou, dead corse, again in complete steel Revisit'st thus the glimpses of the moon.

defs: Finished; ended; concluded; completed; as, the edifice is complete.
quotes: This course of vanity almost complete.

defs: Having all the parts or organs which belong to it or to the typical form; having calyx, corolla, stamens, and pistil.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Completed; p. pr. & vb. n. Completing.]

defs: To bring to a state in which there is no deficiency; to perfect; to consummate; to accomplish; to fulfill; to finish; as, to complete a task, or a poem; to complete a course of education.
quotes: Bred only and completed to the taste Of lustful appetence.
And, to complete her bliss, a fool for mate.
